title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'Small changes, big difference! Write an article explaining how individuals can reduce their environmental footprint in their daily lives. What practical steps would you recommend, and why do you think people are sometimes reluctant to make changes?'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Every Choice Counts: How to Shrink Your Environmental Footprint

The environmental crisis can feel overwhelming, but the good news is that individual actions genuinely do matter — especially when millions of people take them simultaneously.

The most impactful changes are often simpler than people expect. Reducing meat consumption, particularly beef, is one of the most effective steps anyone can take, as livestock farming accounts for a significant proportion of global greenhouse gas emissions. Similarly, choosing public transport or cycling over driving, and switching to a renewable energy supplier, can dramatically reduce your personal carbon footprint.

At home, small habits — such as turning off standby appliances, washing clothes at lower temperatures, and buying second-hand rather than new — all contribute meaningfully over time.

So why are people reluctant to change? Primarily because of convenience, cost concerns, and the mistaken belief that their individual efforts are insignificant. Addressing these misconceptions through better education is essential.

Environmental responsibility is not about perfection — it is about consistent, thoughtful choices. Start small, and build from there.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Sam has recently told you they want to adopt a more eco-friendly lifestyle but does not know where to start. Write an email to Sam with practical suggestions for developing greener habits at home and in everyday life, and encouragement to get started.

Write your email to Sam.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Sam,

It is fantastic to hear that you want to live more sustainably — honestly, it is much easier than it sounds once you get started, and it feels really good too!

My first suggestion would be to focus on food. Try swapping a few meals per week to plant-based options — you do not have to go fully vegetarian, but even small reductions in meat consumption make a real difference. Buying local and seasonal produce is also a brilliant habit to develop.

At home, make a habit of switching off lights and appliances when they are not in use, and try washing clothes at 30 degrees rather than 60 — it uses significantly less energy. Investing in a reusable water bottle and coffee cup will also cut down on unnecessary plastic waste straightaway.

If you want to go further, consider looking into second-hand shopping for clothes and furniture. It saves money as well as resources!

Do not try to change everything at once — pick two or three habits and build from there. You will be amazed by how quickly it becomes second nature.

Let me know how it goes!

Best,
Jordan

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on an environmental blog:

'Help our readers make greener choices! Write a review of an eco-themed documentary, or a green product or service you have used. What did you think of it, and would you recommend it to others who want to live more sustainably?'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Our Planet (Netflix) — Essential Viewing for Anyone Who Cares About the Environment

Produced in collaboration with the WWF and narrated with quiet gravitas by Sir David Attenborough, Our Planet is without doubt one of the most visually stunning and emotionally powerful nature documentaries ever made.

Unlike traditional wildlife series, Our Planet explicitly links its breathtaking footage to the consequences of human activity on natural ecosystems. Each episode focuses on a different habitat — from frozen worlds to coastal seas — and confronts viewers directly with the reality of what is being lost.

The cinematography is extraordinary, and the science is presented accessibly without ever becoming preachy or simplified. The series manages to be both devastating and deeply inspiring, ending each episode with a message of cautious hope and practical possibility.

If I had one criticism, it is that some sequences are almost unbearably distressing, and younger viewers may find certain scenes upsetting.

Nevertheless, I would unreservedly recommend Our Planet to anyone with even a passing interest in the natural world or environmental issues. It is the kind of documentary that changes how you see the world.
